headline: Should we name heatwaves like we name hurricanes to help better prepare for them?
description: As France faces an unusually intense and early heatwave, some scientists and activists are calling for these episodes to be named in order to raise awareness of their dangers. But the idea isn't universally supported.

headline: Trump enforces 'America First' doctrine on emergency food aid
'A reckless, racist, incompetent and treasonous': Bruce Springsteen delivers scathing indictment of Trump at Washington concert
Russian drone hits apartment building in NATO-member Romania, injuring two people
description: Although Romania has detected dozens of drone incursions since Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, this marks the first time one has struck a residential building.

headline: Israel and Russia added to UN blacklist on sexual violence in conflicts
description: The list is published annually by the United Nations, and includes dozens of state and non-state groups credibly suspected of engaging in systematic sexual violence.

headline: Why German trains are never on time anymore
description: The German rail network has deteriorated over the past 20 years, to the point that Deutsche Bahn has become a source of national embarrassment.

headline: 'Paris must remain a city that knows how to celebrate major events'
description: In response to the Paris Police Prefecture's announcement that there will be no parade on the Champs-Elysées if PSG wins the Champions League final on Saturday, essayist Adrien Motel says he regrets that the French capital too often views celebrations and as a potential threat.

description: In an interview, Stéphane Séjourné, executive vice president of the European Commission, raises concerns over Beijing's aggressive competition and calls on EU member states to strengthen their trade defense tools.

headline: How much is your cat's life worth?
description: The 80 million pets in France are sometimes compared to overindulged children. Food, medical care, level of attachment: Is it reasonable to treat your pet like royalty?

headline: 'Do you realize that your statements are completely absurd?': Inside the arrest of the Louvre burglars
description: In their new book, journalists Jean-Michel Décugis, Nicolas-Charles Torrent and Le Monde's Jérémie Pham-Lê recount the events of the burglary at the Paris museum on October 19, 2025, and the arrest of the suspects. Here are excerpts from the book.
byline: Jean-Michel Décugis,  Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Nicolas-Charles Torrent
